mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower real borrowing costs increase net present value of corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% provides room for easing signal activation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2% is near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising UNRATE, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 724131M and HOUST at 1324K show moderate demand levels
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized consumption and housing weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 3-month moving average signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings upward in response to supply strength.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.88 indicates above-trend industrial activity
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 5-day forward returns during INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100k jobs per month signals labor market softening and reduced consumer income growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as consumption growth slows and recession probability rises.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 50k jobs for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159517K shows robust employment levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3% year-over-year) and restrictive federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals monetary policy tightness that constrains equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rates remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 320.32 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% near trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic expansion and rising corporate earnings. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward in an expansion regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23526.09B indicates solid economic activity
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply-side and labor market deterioration. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk increases and earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50k jobs simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.88 and PAYEMS at 159517K show no current weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances.
